titleStmt.title: The Ambassador in Australia ( (Jarman) ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Ambassador in Australia ( Jarman ) to the Secretary of State

opener.dateline:
Canberra , December 31, 1949—1 p. m.
[Received December 31—5:11 a. m.]

div.p: 282. External Affairs official told FosterAndrew B. Foster, Counselor of Embassy in Australia. late Friday (Embtel December 30, 3 p. m.Not printed.) that Australian Government has taken definite decision re recognition Communists’ Government China and has communicated decision to London.
Official added that External Affairs not now at liberty reveal decision to US, but would ask Spender’sPercy C. Spender, Australian Minister for External Affairs and External Territories. permission.

Delayed sending this message hoping to receive information. Definitely refused this morning. Private and confidential information secured from Office New Zealand High Commissioner to effect Australian Government decision taken before Christmas was to note UK recognition on January 2 without approval and not to recognize now; this apparently in line with NZ action.
